title: Future Tense
description: Make plans and talk about what will happen — with feminine -gi endings.
culturalNote: Hindi future tense is one of the clearest gender markers. '-Gi' is feminine, '-ga' is masculine. 'Main jauungi' vs 'main jauunga' — one word tells people your gender. Delhi people use future tense constantly for plans: 'kal milenge?' = shall we meet tomorrow?

exercises.note: 'Main jauungi' = I will go. 'Main kal market jauungi' = I'll go to the market tomorrow. Masculine: 'jauunga'. The -gi ending is the feminine future marker. 'Kahan jauungi tum?' = where will you go? (informal)
'Main karuungi' = I will do it. 'Main zaroor karuungi' = I'll definitely do it. Masculine: 'karuunga'. 'Kya tum yeh karuungi?' = will you do this? A very useful promise phrase.

'Main aauungi' = I will come. 'Main kal zaroor aauungi' = I'll definitely come tomorrow. Masculine: 'aauunga'. 'Kab aauungi tum?' = when will you come? 'Pakka aauungi' = I'll come for sure (a common reassurance).

exercises.prompt: Your friend asks 'Will you come to the party?' You say yes. What do you reply?
exercises.hint: Future tense, feminine — I will come

exercises.note: 'Main baad mein khaauungi' = I'll eat later. 'Kya khaauungi aaj?' = what shall I eat today? Masculine: 'khaauunga'. Common at meal times: 'Abhi nahi khaauungi' = I won't eat right now.

'Main usse bolungi' = I'll tell her/him. 'Main sach bolungi' = I'll tell the truth. Masculine: 'bolunga'. 'Main baad mein bolungi' = I'll say it later. Used for both speaking AND telling someone something.

'Kal milenge?' = shall we meet tomorrow? 'Zaroor milenge' = we'll definitely meet. This is the PLURAL future form — used for 'we' or as a polite 'you will'. It's the go-to phrase for making plans in Delhi. Gender-neutral in plural.

exercises.sentence: Tension mat le, main kal _____. Promise.
exercises.sentenceEnglish: Don't stress, I will come tomorrow. Promise.

exercises.note: Negative future: just add 'nahi' before the verb. 'Main wahan nahi jauungi' = I'm not going there. 'Main yeh nahi karuungi' = I won't do this. 'Bilkul nahi jauungi' = I absolutely won't go.

'Main sochungi' = I'll think about it (common non-committal answer). 'Dekh lena, sochungi' = we'll see, I'll think. Masculine: 'sochunga'. Very useful when you don't want to say yes or no immediately.

exercises.prompt: You want to say 'I will not do this.' Which is correct?
distractors: Main yeh nahi kar rahi hoon

exercises.instruction: Match the masculine future to its feminine form

exercises.sentence: Main kal office nahi _____. Ghar se kaam karuungi.
exercises.sentenceEnglish: I will not go to office tomorrow. I will work from home.
exercises.hint: I will not go — feminine future negative

exercises.prompt: You want to make plans to meet a friend. What's the most natural thing to say?
exercises.hint: 'Milenge' = let's meet / shall we meet — common plan-making phrase
